prevalence.trend: increasing due to rising NAFLD and alcohol consumption

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: INASL guidelines, FIB-4 score, elastography when available

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: INASL guidelines, clinical + ultrasound common, limited biopsy/elastography
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hepatitis B treatment (tenofovir), alcohol cessation, NAFLD lifestyle intervention, limited DAA access for hepatitis C
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High hepatitis E burden (unique to India), rising NAFLD/NASH, alcohol-related cirrhosis, limited transplant access
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National Viral Hepatitis Control Program (NVHCP), affordable generic antivirals, Ayurvedic hepatoprotectives
